function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information. Returns up to 5 results by default with titles, URLs, and descriptions. The query is passed through to the configured backend, so operators such as site:domain, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, and "exact phrase" may work when the backend supports them.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web. You may include backend-supported operators such as site:example.com, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, or "exact phrase".

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 5.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

**Live:** https://nexa.paragu-ai.com  
**Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay  
**Docker service:** nexa_web (1 replica)

## Core Architecture

- **Framework:** `@ai-whisperers/*` package ecosystem (client imports via npm `file:` links)
- **Pages Router** (Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 5)
- **Content:** JSON-driven — `content/es.json`, `nexa-pages/*.json`, `images.json`, `site.json`
- **Loading:** `src/lib/loader.ts` with 60s in-memory TTL cache (replaces raw `readFileSync`)
- **Section map:** 26 components in `SECTION_MAP` (see `docs/02-site/COMPONENT_REGISTRY.md`)
- **Types:** 30+ interfaces in `src/types.ts` define the full data contract
- **Styling:** `src/theme.ts` — 20+ color tokens, all inline styles reference this

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `content/es.json` | All text content (100KB, 15+ page sections) |
| `content/en.json`, `nl.json`, `de.json` | Multi-locale content (currently only ES served) |
| `nexa-pages/*.json` | Page configs — section ordering + content key references |
| `images.json` | Image manifest — 82 entries across 14 categories |
| `site.json` | Domain, features, booking URL, social links |
| `src/pages/[slug].tsx` | Main page renderer — SSR, SECTION_MAP dispatch |
| `src/pages/index.tsx` | Homepage renderer (separate file) |
| `src/pages/blog/[slug].tsx` | Individual blog post renderer — MDX-driven SSR |
| `src/lib/loader.ts` | Shared JSON loader with 60s cache |
| `src/types.ts` | 30+ TypeScript interfaces |
| `src/theme.ts` | Brand design tokens |
| `scripts/screenshot-all.mjs` | Full-page screenshot automation — builds, starts server, captures 22 pages |
| `docs/06-marketing/testimonials-google-form.md` | Google Form template for collecting client testimonials |
| `content/blog/posts-en.json` | English full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `content/blog/posts-nl.json` | Dutch full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `content/blog/posts-de.json` | German full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `blog/en/*.mdx` | 16 English MDX blog articles (6 originals + 10 translated from ES) |
| `blog/nl/*.mdx` | 11 Dutch MDX blog articles (1 original + 10 translated from ES) |
| `blog/de/*.mdx` | 10 German MDX blog articles (translated from ES) |

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build
npm run screenshots      # Full-page screenshot automation (22 pages → screenshots/<iter>/)
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod --no-cache .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
```

Requires `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` for `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` (if building with unpublished packages).

## Documentation (docs/)

| Category | Contents |
|----------|----------|
| `00-architecture/` | Core framework, bridge points, data flow, standards |
| `01-client/` | Stakeholder info, intake, questionnaire responses |
| `02-site/` | Architecture, audit, improvement plan, DNS, component registry |
| `03-brand/` | Brand guide, tokens, image prompts, social assets |
| `04-images/` | Image manifest (111 images), prompt library |
| `05-content/` | Locales, blog catalog, editorial calendar |
| `06-marketing/` | Comparisons, email nurture, FAQ, lead magnets, ads, testimonials |
| `07-seo/` | Keyword strategy, content gaps |
| `08-integrations/` | HubSpot, Mailchimp, GA4, WhatsApp AI bridge |
| `09-market-intelligence/` | Solstein analysis, market sizing, competition, financial model |
| `10-deployment/` | Deployment runbook, CI/CD |
| `11-launch/` | Launch runbook, pre-launch checklist |
| `12-factory/` | NEW_CLIENT_BOOTSTRAP.md — template for creating new client repos |

## Critical Notes

- `is_demo: true` in site.json — flip when placeholders replaced
- Team portraits, testimonials, and stock imagery are AI placeholders as of May 2026
- Nexa's primary domain (nexaparaguay.com) still points to Shopify — needs DNS A record to 72.61.44.159
- GA4 measurement ID: G-XE49GLEP34
- See `docs/00-architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` for the Core-to-Client bridge explanation

## WhatsApp AI Bot Setup

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| Bridge URL | https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud |
| Nexa client instance | `client-nexa-paraguay-5138` (phone: 595982515138) |
| Mode | `ventas` (sales) — auto-qualifies leads |
| Personality | Loaded with full FAQ, programs, tax data, qualification flow |
| Evolution instance | `nexa-paraguay` |
| QR scan image | `public/qr-nexa-whatsapp.png` |
| **NEXT STEP** | Team must scan QR from WhatsApp (Settings → Linked Devices) |

After QR scan: AI responds in EN/NL/DE/ES, qualifies leads (hot/warm/cold), books consultations. See `docs/08-integrations/whatsapp-ai-bridge.md`.

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Local:** /root/elviajero
- **Docker service:** elviajero_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`) || Host(`viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter. Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) — shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero --with-registry-auth
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

{"total_count": 2, "files": ["/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/package.json", "/root/paragu-ai-builder/package.json"]} [Subdirectory context discovered: ~/paragu-ai-builder/AGENTS.md] # Paragu-AI Builder - AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. {"content": "     1|const fs = require('fs')
     2|const path = require('path')
     3|
     4|interface CacheEntry { data: any; timestamp: number }
     5|const cache = new Map<string, CacheEntry>()
     6|const TTL = 60_000
     7|
     8|export function loadJSON(dir: string, file: string): any {
     9|  const fullPath = path.join(dir, file)
    10|  const now = Date.now()
    11|  const cached = cache.get(fullPath)
    12|  if (cached && (now - cached.timestamp) < TTL) return cached.data
    13|  try {
    14|    const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8'))
    15|    cache.set(fullPath, { data, timestamp: now })
    16|    return data
    17|  } catch { return null }
    18|}
    19|
    20|export function loadContentJSON(dir: string, file: string): any {
    21|  const fullPath = path.join(dir, file)
    22|  const cached = cache.get(fullPath)
    23|  if (cached && (Date.now() - cached.timestamp) < TTL) return cached.data
    24|  const data = loadJSON(dir, file)
    25|  if (data) cache.set(fullPath, { data, timestamp: Date.now() })
    26|  return data
    27|}

{"content": "     1|/**
     2| * Legacy section renderer — kept for backwards compatibility with
     3| * demo/[business]/ routes. Delegates to the auto-generated section map.
     4| * New tenants should use site-renderer.tsx instead.
     5| */
     6|import { COMPONENTS } from './site-renderer'
     7|import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'
     8|import { resolveSectionAlias } from './section-registry'
     9|import type { ComposedSection } from './compose'
    10|
    11|export function renderSection(section: ComposedSection): React.ReactNode {
    12|  const kebab = resolveSectionAlias(section.type)
    13|  const Component = COMPONENTS[kebab]
    14|  if (!Component) {
    15|    logger.warn('Unknown section type — skipping render', {
    16|      action: 'renderSection',
    17|      sectionType: section.type,
    18|      normalized: kebab,
    19|      order: section.order,
    20|    })
    21|    return null
    22|  }
    23|  return <Component key={`${kebab}-${section.order}`} {...section.data} variant={section.data.variant as string | undefined} />
    24|}
    25|
    26|export function renderSections(sections: ComposedSection[]): React.ReactNode[] {
    27|  return sections.sort((a, b) => a.order - b.order).map(renderSection).filter(Boolean) as React.ReactNode[]
    28|}

{"content": "     1|import type { ResolvedPage } from './site-types'
     2|import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'
     3|import { GENERATED_MAP as _RAW_MAP } from './generated/renderer-map'
     4|
     5|const GENERATED_MAP = _RAW_MAP as Record<string, React.ComponentType<any>>
     6|import { SectionWrapper } from '@/components/ui/section-wrapper'
     7|import { WhatsAppFloat } from '@/components/sections/navigation/whatsapp-float'
     8|import { ProductCatalogSection } from '@/components/sections/commerce/product-catalog-section'
     9|import { SectionErrorBoundary } from '@/components/ui/section-error-boundary'
    10|
    11|// GENERATED_MAP is auto-generated but may miss some edge-case components
    12|// (e.g. files named without -section suffix where export name differs).
    13|// These hardcoded overrides paper over gaps until the generator is fixed.
    14|const OVERRIDES: Record<string, React.ComponentType<any>> = {
    15|  'whatsapp-float': WhatsAppFloat,
    16|  // Generated file maps product-catalog to buildWhatsAppUrl (a utility fn),
    17|  // not the actual React component. Override until generator is fixed.
    18|  'product-catalog': ProductCatalogSection,
    19|}
    20|
    21|const { 'product-catalog': _pc, ...CLEAN_MAP } = GENERATED_MAP as Record<string, React.ComponentType<any>>
    22|
    23|export const COMPONENTS: Record<string, React.ComponentType<any>> = {
    24|  ...CLEAN_MAP,
    25|  ...OVERRIDES,
    26|}
    27|
    28|export function renderPage(page: ResolvedPage): React.ReactNode {
    29|  return page.sections.map((s, i) => {
    30|    const C = COMPONENTS[s.id]
    31|    if (!C) {
    32|      logger.warn('No component bound for section — skipping render', {
    33|        action: 'renderPage',
    34|        sectionId: s.id,
    35|        index: i,
    36|      })
    37|      return null
    38|    }
    39|
    40|    const props = {
    41|      ...s.props,
    42|      variant: s.variant,
    43|      locale: page.locale,
    44|    } as Record<string, unknown>
    45|
    46|    // Auto-alternate backgrounds for consecutive default sections
    47|    // to create visual depth. Pattern: default → alt → surface-light
    48|    // dark themes need more differentiation so we add subtle border dividers
    49|    const bgCycle = i % 3 === 0 ? 'default' as const : i % 3 === 1 ? 'alt' as const : 'surface-light' as const
    50|    const styling = s.styling
    51|      ? { ...s.styling, background: (s.styling.background || bgCycle) }
    52|      : { background: bgCycle }
    53|    
    54|    // Add subtle top border on alternating sections for dark themes
    55|    // Creates visible separation without relying solely on background color
    56|    if (page.theme.isDark && i > 0 && bgCycle !== 'default') {
    57|      styling.border = 'top'
    58|    }
    59|
    60|    return (
    61|      <SectionErrorBoundary key={`seb-${s.id}-${i}`} sectionId={s.id} index={i}>
    62|        <SectionWrapper key={`wrap-${s.id}-${i}`} styling={styling} id={s.id}>
    63|          <C {...props} />
    64|        </SectionWrapper>
    65|      </SectionErrorBoundary>
    66|    )
    67|  })
    68|}

{"content": "     1|/**
     2| * Token Resolver - converts JSON design tokens to CSS custom properties.
     3| *
     4| * Pipeline: src/tokens/[type].tokens.json → merge with base → CSS variables
     5| *
     6| * Each business type has its own token file with palettes, typography, and
     7| * component overrides. The resolver merges type tokens with base tokens
     8| * and flattens them into a CSS string for injection into the page.
     9| */
    10|
    11|import type { BusinessType } from '@/lib/types'
    12|import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'fs'
    13|import { resolve } from 'path'
    14|
    15|export interface TokenColors {
    16|  primary: string
    17|  secondary: string
    18|  accent?: string
    19|  background: string
    20|  surface: string
    21|  text: string
    22|  textLight?: string
    23|  textMuted?: string
    24|  success?: string
    25|  error?: string
    26|  warning?: string
    27|  surfaceLight?: string
    28|  secondaryHover?: string
    29|}
    30|
    31|export interface TokenTypography {
    32|  heading: string
    33|  body: string
    34|  accent?: string
    35|  headingWeight: string
    36|  bodyWeight: string
    37|  textTransform?: string
    38|}
    39|
    40|export interface TypeTokens {
    41|  name: string
    42|  extends: string
    43|  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    44|  mood: string[]
    45|  palettes: Record<string, { name: string; colors: TokenColors }>
    46|  defaultPalette: string
    47|  typography: TokenTypography
    48|  googleFonts: string[]
    49|  borderRadius: string
    50|  buttonStyle: string
    51|  components: Record<string, Record<string, unknown>>
    52|}
    53|
    54|export interface BaseTokens {
    55|  spacing: Record<string, { value: string }>
    56|  borderRadius: Record<string, { value: string }>
    57|  shadows: Record<string, { value: string }>
    58|  typography: {
    59|    scale: Record<string, { value: string }>
    60|    lineHeight: Record<string, { value: string }>
    61|  }
    62|  animation: {
    63|    duration: Record<string, { value: string }>
    64|  }
    65|  layout: Record<string, Record<string, { value: string }>>
    66|  semanticColors?: Record<string, { value: string }>
    67|}
    68|
    69|export interface ResolvedTokens {
    70|  cssVariables: Record<string, string>
    71|  cssString: string
    72|  googleFontsUrl: string
    73|  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    74|}
    75|
    76|// See resolve-site-tokens.ts — same rationale.
    77|const PROJECT_ROOT = (() => {
    78|  if (process.env.SRC_DIR) return resolve(process.env.SRC_DIR, '..')
    79|  const cwd = process.cwd()
    80|  return existsSync(resolve(cwd, 'src')) ? cwd : resolve(cwd, '..')
    81|})()
    82|
    83|function loadJsonFile<T>(relativePath: string): T {
    84|  const fullPath = resolve(PROJECT_ROOT, relativePath)
    85|  const content = readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8')
    86|  return JSON.parse(content)
    87|}
    88|
    89|import { verticalFolder } from '@/lib/verticals-catalog'
    90|import { getRegistry } from '@/lib/engine/static-config'
    91|
    92|/**
    93| * Load type tokens. Three paths, in order:
    94| *
    95| * 1. The type has its own concrete token file with palettes → use it directly.
    96| * 2. The type has a thin wrapper (`extends: "vertical:<id>"`) → merge the
    97| *    wrapper overrides onto the vertical defaults.
    98| * 3. The type token file doesn't exist at all (the common case after we
    99| *    eliminated the ~1,800 wrapper stubs) → look up the verticalId from the
   100| *    registry entry and load the vertical defaults straight.
   101| *
   102| * Every path resolves the on-disk folder via `verticalFolder()` which consults
   103| * the single vertical catalog at src/verticals/catalog.json.
   104| */
   105|function loadTypeTokens(businessType: BusinessType): TypeTokens {
   106|  let raw: (Partial<TypeTokens> & { extends?: string }) | null = null
   107|  try {
   108|    raw = loadJsonFile<Partial<TypeTokens> & { extends?: string }>(
   109|      `src/tokens/${businessType}.tokens.json`,
   110|    )
   111|  } catch {
   112|    // No per-type file — fall through to vertical defaults via registry lookup.
   113|  }
   114|
   115|  // Wrapper pattern: explicit `extends: "vertical:<id>"` → merge.
   116|  if (raw?.extends?.startsWith('vertical:')) {
   117|    const verticalId = raw.extends.slice('vertical:'.length)
   118|    const verticalDefaults = loadJsonFile<TypeTokens>(
   119|      `src/verticals/${verticalFolder(verticalId)}/defaults.tokens.json`,
   120|    )
   121|    return { ...verticalDefaults, ...raw, extends: raw.extends } as TypeTokens
   122|  }
   123|
   124|  // Concrete palette file present → use as-is.
   125|  if (raw?.palettes) return raw as TypeTokens
   126|
   127|  // No token file at all — derive vertical from the registry entry.
   128|  const registry = getRegistry(businessType) as { verticalId?: string } | null
   129|  if (!registry?.verticalId) {
   130|    throw new Error(
   131|      `[TokenResolver] No tokens found for "${businessType}" and no verticalId in registry. Create src/tokens/${businessType}.tokens.json or assign verticalId in registry.`,
   132|    )
   133|  }
   134|  return loadJsonFile<TypeTokens>(
   135|    `src/verticals/${verticalFolder(registry.verticalId)}/defaults.tokens.json`,
   136|  )
   137|}
   138|
   139|/**
   140| * Resolve tokens for a business type into CSS custom properties.
   141| */
   142|export function resolveTokens(
   143|  businessType: BusinessType,
   144|  paletteOverride?: string
   145|): ResolvedTokens {
   146|  const base = loadJsonFile<BaseTokens>('src/tokens/base.tokens.json')
   147|  const type = loadTypeTokens(businessType)
   148|
   149|  const paletteName = paletteOverride || type.defaultPalette
   150|  const palette = type.palettes[paletteName]
   151|  if (!palette) {
   152|    throw new Error(`[TokenResolver] Palette "${paletteName}" not found for ${businessType}`)
   153|  }
   154|  const colors = palette.colors
   155|  const pc = colors as unknown as Record<string, string | undefined>
   156|
   157|  const vars: Record<string, string> = {}
   158|
   159|  // Colors from type palette. Foregrounds are overridable per palette —
   160|  // without them, dark themes default to `colors.text`, which breaks when
   161|  // `primary` is also a light color (white text on light bg = invisible).
   162|  vars['--primary'] = colors.primary
   163|  vars['--primary-foreground'] = pc.primaryForeground || (type.theme === 'dark' ? colors.text : '#ffffff')
   164|  vars['--secondary'] = colors.secondary
   165|  vars['--secondary-foreground'] = pc.secondaryForeground || '#ffffff'
   166|  vars['--accent'] = colors.accent || colors.secondary
   167|  vars['--accent-foreground'] = pc.accentForeground || colors.text
   168|  vars['--background'] = colors.background
   169|  vars['--surface'] = colors.surface
   170|  vars['--surface-light'] = colors.surfaceLight || colors.surface
   171|  vars['--text'] = colors.text
   172|  vars['--text-light'] = colors.textLight || colors.textMuted || '#666666'
   173|  vars['--text-muted'] = colors.textMuted || colors.textLight || '#888888'
   174|  vars['--success'] = colors.success || '#4a7c59'
   175|  vars['--error'] = colors.error || '#c0392b'
   176|  vars['--warning'] = colors.warning || '#f39c12'
   177|  vars['--secondary-hover'] = colors.secondaryHover || colors.secondary
   178|
   179|  // Typography
   180|  vars['--font-heading'] = type.typography.heading
   181|  vars['--font-body'] = type.typography.body
   182|  vars['--font-accent'] = type.typography.accent || type.typography.body
   183|  vars['--heading-weight'] = type.typography.headingWeight
   184|  vars['--body-weight'] = type.typography.bodyWeight
   185|  vars['--heading-transform'] = type.typography.textTransform || 'none'
   186|
   187|  // Spacing from base
   188|  for (const [key, token] of Object.entries(base.spacing)) {
   189|    vars[`--spacing-${key}`] = token.value
   190|  }
   191|
   192|  // Border radius from base
   193|  for (const [key, token] of Object.entries(base.borderRadius)) {
   194|    vars[`--radius-${key}`] = token.value
   195|  }
   196|
   197|  // Shadows from base
   198|  vars['--shadow-card'] = base.shadows.card.value
   199|  vars['--shadow-card-hover'] = base.shadows.cardHover.value
   200|  vars['--shadow-button'] = base.shadows.button.value
   201|  vars['--shadow-nav'] = base.shadows.nav.value
   202|
   203|  // Semantic colors (success/error/warning/info). Types can override by
   204|  // declaring their own `semanticColors` block; otherwise they inherit
   205|  // these neutral defaults.
   206|  if (base.semanticColors) {
   207|    for (const [key, token] of Object.entries(base.semanticColors)) {
   208|      // Camel → kebab for css (successSurface → success-surface)
   209|      const cssKey = key.replace(/[A-Z]/g, (c) => `-${c.toLowerCase()}`)
   210|      vars[`--color-${cssKey}`] = token.value
   211|    }
   212|  }
   213|  const typeSemantic = (type as { semanticColors?: Record<string, { value: string } | string> }).semanticColors
   214|  if (typeSemantic) {
   215|    for (const [key, token] of Object.entries(typeSemantic)) {
   216|      const cssKey = key.replace(/[A-Z]/g, (c) => `-${c.toLowerCase()}`)
   217|      const value = typeof token === 'string' ? token : token.value
   218|      vars[`--color-${cssKey}`] = value
   219|    }
   220|  }
   221|
   222|  // Typography scale from base
   223|  for (const [key, token] of Object.entries(base.typography.scale)) {
   224|    vars[`--text-${key}`] = token.value
   225|  }
   226|  for (const [key, token] of Object.entries(base.typography.lineHeight)) {
   227|    vars[`--leading-${key}`] = token.value
   228|  }
   229|
   230|  // Animation from base
   231|  vars['--transition-fast'] = base.animation.duration.fast.value
   232|  vars['--transition-normal'] = base.animation.duration.default.value
   233|  vars['--transition-slow'] = base.animation.duration.slow.value
   234|
   235|  // Layout from base
   236|  if (base.layout?.grid) {
   237|    vars['--grid-gap'] = base.layout.grid.gap.value
   238|  }
   239|
   240|  // Button component overrides from type
   241|  if (type.components?.button) {
   242|    const btn = type.components.button
   243|    if (btn.borderRadius) vars['--button-radius'] = btn.borderRadius as string
   244|    if (btn.padding) vars['--button-padding'] = btn.padding as string
   245|    if (btn.textTransform) vars['--button-transform'] = btn.textTransform as string
   246|    if (btn.fontWeight) vars['--button-weight'] = btn.fontWeight as string
   247|  }
   248|
   249|  // Google Fonts URL
   250|  const googleFontsUrl = type.googleFonts.length > 0
   251|    ? `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?${type.googleFonts.map((f) => `family=${f}`).join('&')}&display=swap`
   252|    : ''
   253|
   254|  // Build CSS string
   255|  const cssString = Object.entries(vars)
   256|    .map(([key, value]) => `${key}: ${value};`)
   257|    .join('\n  ')
   258|
   259|  return {
   260|    cssVariables: vars,
   261|    cssString: `:root {\n  ${cssString}\n}`,
   262|    googleFontsUrl,
   263|    theme: type.theme,
   264|  }
   265|}
   266|
   267|/**
   268| * Get the list of available palettes for a business type.
   269| */
   270|export function getAvailablePalettes(
   271|  businessType: BusinessType
   272|): Array<{ id: string; name: string }> {
   273|  const type = loadJsonFile<TypeTokens>(`src/tokens/${businessType}.tokens.json`)
   274|  return Object.entries(type.palettes).map(([id, palette]) => ({
   275|    id,
   276|    name: palette.name,
   277|  }))
   278|}
   279|", "total_lines": 278, "file_size": 9881, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|/**
     2| * Supported locales across the platform.
     3| * Sites opt into a subset via `site.json.locales`.
     4| *
     5| * Kept in sync with the locales actually shipped by real tenants:
     6| *   - nexa-paraguay → nl, en, de, es
     7| *   - nexa-propiedades → es, en, pt
     8| *   - everyone else → es or en
     9| *
    10| * Before this was extended beyond en/es, the language switcher rendered
    11| * `aria-label="undefined"` for any tenant shipping nl/de/pt because the
    12| * lookup in LOCALE_LABELS fell off the end.
    13| */
    14|export const ALL_LOCALES = ['en', 'es', 'nl', 'de', 'pt'] as const
    15|export type Locale = (typeof ALL_LOCALES)[number]
    16|
    17|export const LOCALE_LABELS: Record<Locale, string> = {
    18|  en: 'English',
    19|  es: 'Español',
    20|  nl: 'Nederlands',
    21|  de: 'Deutsch',
    22|  pt: 'Português',
    23|}
    24|
    25|export const LOCALE_HTML_LANG: Record<Locale, string> = {
    26|  en: 'en-US',
    27|  es: 'es',
    28|  nl: 'nl-NL',
    29|  de: 'de-DE',
    30|  pt: 'pt-BR',
    31|}
    32|
    33|export function isLocale(value: string): value is Locale {
    34|  return (ALL_LOCALES as readonly string[]).includes(value)
    35|}
    36|
    37|export const DEFAULT_LOCALE: Locale = 'es'
    38|", "total_lines": 37, "file_size": 1045, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

    16|ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
    17|RUN npm run build

    19|FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS runner
    20|WORKDIR /app

    22|ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

---

## Complete Architecture Analysis: Paraguayan Professional Services Websites

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Router:** ALL repos use **Next.js App Router** (not Pages Router). The `next.config` always has `output: "standalone"` for Docker deployment.

**Layout patterns (3 tiers):**

| Tier | Structure | Used by |
|------|-----------|---------|
| **A - Multi-tenant Builder** | `web/app/[business]/` + `web/app/s/[locale]/` | paragu-ai-builder |
| **B - Locale-prefixed** | `src/app/[locale]/[slug]/page.tsx` + `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` | nexa-paraguay (the most evolved client) |
| **C - Simple flat** | `app/page.tsx`, `app/servicios/page.tsx`, etc. | elviajero, villamayor, 3md, template-temp |

**Key finding:** Tier B (nexa-paraguay) is the reference for professional services. It uses:
- `/src/app/[locale]/[slug]/page.tsx` — SSG page renderer that loads JSON config
- `/src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` — Homepage (separate renderer)
- `/src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` — MDX blog posts
- `/src/app/layout.tsx` — Root layout (fonts, cookie banner)
- `/src/middleware.ts` — Locale detection (cookie, accept-language, path)

### 2. I18N APPROACH

Two patterns:

**Pattern A: Path-based locales with middleware** (nexa-paraguay)
```
/es/ (default), /en/, /nl/, /de/
```
Middleware auto-detects locale from cookie → accept-language → defaults to 'es'. Pages use `params: { locale }` to load locale-specific JSON content.

**Pattern B: Simple single-locale** (most other repos)
JSON file is just loaded directly from `content/es.json` — no i18n routing or middleware. El Viajero has a language toggle that swaps JSON files.

**Pattern C: Builder's centralized i18n** (paragu-ai-builder)
```typescript
// lib/i18n/config.ts
export const ALL_LOCALES = ['en', 'es', 'nl', 'de', 'pt'] as const
export const LOCALE_LABELS: Record<Locale, string> = { ... }
export function isLocale(value: string): value is Locale { ... }
```
Each site declares `locales: ['es', 'en']` in site.json. The engine resolves locale-specific content per site.

**Best practice for professional services:** Pattern A (nexa-paraguay). Locale-prefixed paths with middleware detection. Content lives in `content/{locale}.json`.

### 3. COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE

**Three distinct architectures:**

**A. Builder's Section Registry System** (paragu-ai-builder — the most sophisticated)
```
components/
  ui/             — 50+ primitives (button, card, dialog, etc.) — CVA-based
  sections/
    navigation/   — header, whatsapp-float, language-selector
    hero/         — hero-section, hero-video
    content/      — features, services, team, process, testimonials, faq
    commerce/     — product-catalog, checkout, cart
    specialty/    — 60+ vertical-specific sections (vet-services, mortgage-calc, etc.)
```
Each section is registered in `SECTION_CATALOG` (1030 lines, ~70 sections). Sections declare `id`, `defaultVariant`, `variants[]`, `requiredContentFields[]`. The engine auto-generates renderer maps.

**B. JSON-driven page composition** (nexa-paraguay)
```
nexa-pages/servicios.json → PageConfig with sections array
content/es.json → All section data
components/SectionsRenderer.tsx → Dispatches to named components
```
Sections referenced by ID string, data extracted via dot-path keys (e.g., `"content": "home.hero"`).

**C. Direct import** (elviajero, template repos)
Pages directly `import content from "@/content/es.json"` and render inline. Simpler but less maintainable for many pages.

**Best practice for professional services:** Pattern B (nexa-paraguay). JSON-driven pages with section dispatch. Types are enforced via `types.ts` (30+ interfaces).

### 4. TAILWIND VERSION AND THEME SYSTEM

**Critical finding: Two Tailwind versions in use**

| Version | Used by |
|---------|---------|
| **Tailwind v4** (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) | nexa-paraguay, bufete-mendez, villamayor-asociados, 3md-website, elviajero, template-temp |
| **Tailwind 3.4.19** (explicit pin) | paragu-ai-builder (locked — "DO NOT upgrade to v4, breaks build") |

**Tailwind v4 config** (all client repos):
```js
// postcss.config.mjs
const config = { plugins: { "@tailwindcss/postcss": {} } }
export default config

// globals.css
@import "tailwindcss";
@theme {
  --color-primary: #1B2A4A;
  // ...
}
```

**Theme system: Two approaches**

**A. CSS Variables from JSON tokens** (paragu-ai-builder — the reference):
```
src/tokens/base.tokens.json       → spacing, shadows, breakpoints, animation
src/tokens/{type}.tokens.json     → palettes (multiple), typography, googleFonts
```
Resolver at `lib/tokens/resolver.ts` merges base + type tokens → CSS `:root` variables:
```css
--primary, --secondary, --background, --surface, --text, --font-heading, etc.
```
All components use `var(--primary)`, never hardcoded colors.

**B. Inline theme.ts** (nexa-paraguay):
```typescript
// src/theme.ts — 82 lines of brand tokens
export const theme = {
  colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', ... },
  radii: { ... },
  shadows: { ... },
  fonts: { body: "'Inter', ...", heading: "'Playfair Display', ..." },
}
```

**Best practice for professional services:** CSS Variables approach (builder's pattern). Each client gets a `tokens.json` file with brand colors. Components reference `var(--primary)`, `var(--background)`, etc.

### 5. DOCKER SETUP

**Standardized Dockerfile pattern (ALL repos):**
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps   # npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder  # npm run build
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner   # USER nextjs, COPY --from=builder /app/.next/standalone ./
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```
Nexa paraguay also copies `content/`, `nexa-pages/`, `images.json` into the runner.

**Docker Compose / Docker Stack (ALL repos):**
```yaml
services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: {name}:prod
    networks: [agent-net]       # external Traefik network
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      resources:
        limits: { cpus: '0.5', memory: 512M }
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{name}.rule=Host(`{subdomain}.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.{name}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.{name}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 15s
```

**Infrastructure pattern:**
```
Cloudflare (DNS + SSL) → VPS (72.61.44.159) → Traefik → service:3000
```
- All containers share `agent-net` (external Traefik overlay network)
- 2 replicas per service for most sites
- 512MB memory limit per container
- Let's Encrypt via Traefik

### 6. DEPLOYMENT STRATEGY

**Two deployment paths:**

**A. Docker Swarm (primary — used by most client sites):**
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t {name}:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {stack_name}
```

**B. Cloudflare Pages (builder's newer approach):**
```bash
# uses @opennextjs/cloudflare
wrangler pages deploy
```
Config: `open-next.config.ts` = `defineCloudflareConfig({})` (default minimal config)

**Deployment script patterns:** No external CI/CD providers found in `.github/workflows/`. Deployments are manual via SSH + Docker commands documented in CLAUDE.md files.

### 7. CONTENT MANAGEMENT APPROACH

**ALL repos are JSON-content-driven.** There is no headless CMS:

| Layer | File | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| Content | `content/{locale}.json` | All text, nav items, section data, SEO metadata |
| Pages | `nexa-pages/*.json` or `pages/*.json` | Section ordering + content key references |
| Images | `images.json` | Image manifest with paths/alt-text |
| Site | `site.json` | Domain, features, contact, social links |
| Tokens | `tokens.json` | Design tokens for branding |

**Content structure** (nexa-paraguay reference):
```json
{
  "siteName": "...",
  "navigation": { "businessName": "...", "navItems": [...], "ctaText": "...", "ctaHref": "..." },
  "footer": { "businessName": "...", "columns": [...], "whatsapp": "...", ... },
  "home": {
    "seo": { "title": "...", "description": "..." },
    "hero": { "headline": "...", "subheadline": "...", "ctaPrimaryText": "...", ... },
    "stats": { ... },
    "services": { ... },

    "process": { ... },
    "testimonials": { ... },
    "contact": { ... },
    "faq": { ... }

  "servicesPage": { ... },
  "contactPage": { ... }
}
```

**Loader with cache** (nexa-paraguay):
```typescript

const TTL = 60_000

### 8. CI/CD PIPELINES

**No automated CI/CD found in repo workflows.** All repos lack `.github/workflows/` directories. The pipeline is:

1. **Manual build on VPS** (or locally)
2. `docker build -t {name}:prod .`
3. `docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml {stack}`
4. Traefik auto-detects new containers on `agent-net`

The builder's package.json has extensive validation scripts but they're run manually:
```bash
npm run validate:all   # validates sites, content, legal, contrast, schemas
npm run check          # typecheck + lint + format check
npm run test:ci        # vitest + playwright
```

**Commit quality:** builder has `husky` + `commitlint` + `lint-staged` (conventional commits enforced).

### 9. SECTION/BLOCK COMPONENT PATTERNS

**The builder has the definitive pattern** — `paragu-ai-builder/web/lib/engine/section-registry.ts` catalogs ~70 sections:

**Section categories:**
```
navigation:    header, footer, whatsapp-float, language-selector, menu
hero:          hero (3 variants), hero-video
content:       features, services, team, testimonials, faq, process, trust-signals
commerce:      product-catalog, featured-products, commerce-catalog, cart

```

**Key design pattern — SectionWrapper:**

// - Padding (none/sm/md/lg/xl)

## Summary for Paraguayan Professional Services (Notaries)

**The recommended stack for a new professional services site:**

| Aspect | Recommendation |
|--------|---------------|
| **Framework** | Next.js 16 (App Router) + TypeScript 5 |
| **Routing** | `app/[locale]/[slug]/page.tsx` pattern (nexa-paraguay) |
| **Tailwind** | v4 (unless using builder, which locks to v3.4.19) |
| **Theme** | CSS variables from `tokens.json` (builder pattern) |
| **Content** | JSON-driven: `content/es.json` + `pages/*.json` |
| **Docker** | 3-stage alpine build, `output: "standalone"` |
| **Deploy** | Docker Swarm → Traefik on VPS (72.61.44.159) |
| **Components** | Section registry pattern with typed props |
| **SEO** | generateMetadata, JSON-LD, dynamic sitemap, security headers |
| **i18n** | Path-based with middleware (at minimum ES, possibly EN) |
| **Auth** | None for static sites; Supabase Auth if e-commerce needed |
| **DB** | None for static; Supabase if dynamic data needed |
| **CI/CD** | Manual Docker deploy (no GH Actions found) |

**Key differences for a notary/law firm site** (bufete-mendez is the closest reference):
- Schema type: `ProfessionalService` or `LegalService`
- Section order: hero → features/services → trust-signals → team → process → testimonials → faq → contact
- Key components: header, hero, services, trust-signals, team, process, testimonials, faq, booking-embed, contact, footer, whatsapp-float
- Content: practice areas, attorney profiles, case results, fee structure, consultation booking
- No e-commerce, no database, no user accounts
